title: Simplify install.sh: hardcode repo, remove dead schedule code, drop all sed
body:
## Context

Running `install.sh` on macOS fails with:
```
sed: 1: ".github/workflows/versi ...": invalid command code .
```

BSD sed requires `sed -i ''` vs GNU sed's `sed -i`. But deeper analysis shows the sed calls are either dead code (schedule substitution against templates that don't have the placeholder) or unnecessary complexity (SEMVER_REPO param for hypothetical forks).

## Acceptance Criteria

### install.sh changes
- [ ] Remove the `SEMVER_REPO` parameter — script takes only `<version>`
- [ ] Remove all schedule/cron code (lines 52-70: read/validate, lines 88-91: sed substitute) — dead code, templates have no schedule placeholder
- [ ] Remove all `sed` calls from `install_workflow()` — no substitution needed
- [ ] Update usage comment and header to reflect single-param interface
- [ ] `install_workflow()` just fetches the template, no post-processing

### Template changes
- [ ] `templates/github/workflows/install-git-semver.yml`: change `SEMVER_REPO: USER/git-semver` to `SEMVER_REPO: mangimangi/git-semver`

### README changes
- [ ] Remove `install.schedule` from config docs (line ~165, ~209)
- [ ] Remove "Runs weekly to check for updates" mention (line ~28)
- [ ] Simplify install command examples to `bash -s <version>` (drop repo param)

### Workflow changes
- [ ] Update `.github/workflows/install-git-semver.yml` (the repo's own workflow) to pass only version to install.sh (drop repo param from `bash -s` call)
- [ ] Update `.github/workflows/install-on-release.yml` if it also passes repo param

### Tests
- [ ] Add `tests/test_install.sh` (shell tests) or `tests/test_install.py` (pytest) that verify:
  - install.sh creates `.semver/git-semver`, `.semver/.version`, `.semver/config.json`
  - install.sh installs workflow templates when not present
  - install.sh skips workflow templates when already present
  - Installed workflow templates contain `mangimangi/git-semver` (not placeholder)

title: Create .semver/bump-and-release script with tests
body:
## Summary

Move all orchestration logic from `version-bump.yml` into a new `.semver/bump-and-release` Python script. The workflow currently contains ~100 lines of inline bash handling config reading, 4 conditional bump modes (push/dispatch × automerge/PR), PR creation, and release creation. All of this should live in an updatable script so the workflow can be installed once and never modified.

## Design

**Separate script** (not a git-semver subcommand) — keeps `git-semver` CI-agnostic. Follows existing pattern (`.vendored/install`, `.vendored/check`, `.dogfood/resolve`).

The script receives GitHub context via environment variables:
- `GITHUB_EVENT_NAME` — "push" or "workflow_dispatch" (built-in GHA var)
- `GITHUB_EVENT_BEFORE` — the before SHA for push events (workflow passes this)
- `GITHUB_SHA` — current commit SHA (built-in GHA var)
- `INPUT_BUMP_TYPE` — patch/minor/major (workflow passes from inputs)
- `INPUT_SUBDIRECTORY` — optional subdir (workflow passes from inputs)
- `INPUT_CHANGELOG_DESCRIPTION` — optional changelog text (workflow passes from inputs)
- `GH_TOKEN` — GitHub token (set by workflow)
- `GITHUB_REPOSITORY` — owner/repo (built-in GHA var)

### Logic to move from workflow

1. **Read config** — extract `install.on_merge` and `install.automerge` from `.semver/config.json`
2. **Git user config** — set github-actions[bot] name/email
3. **Push + automerge=true** — `.semver/git-semver bump-all --since $BEFORE`
4. **Push + automerge=false** — bump-all --no-push, create PR if changes detected
5. **Dispatch + automerge=true** — `.semver/git-semver bump $TYPE [--subdir] [--description]`
6. **Dispatch + automerge=false** — bump --no-push, create PR
7. **Release creation** — iterate tags at HEAD, `gh release create` for each

### Exit behavior
- Exit 0 on success (including "nothing to bump")
- Exit 1 on error

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.semver/bump-and-release` script exists and is executable
- [ ] Handles all 4 bump modes (push/dispatch × automerge/PR) matching current workflow behavior exactly
- [ ] Handles release creation for tags at HEAD
- [ ] Reads config from `.semver/config.json` for on_merge/automerge
- [ ] Respects on_merge=false (skips push-triggered bumps)
- [ ] Sets git user config (github-actions[bot])
- [ ] New test file `tests/test_bump_and_release.py` covering:
  - Config reading (on_merge/automerge defaults and overrides)
  - Push + automerge mode (calls bump-all with --since)
  - Push + PR mode (calls bump-all --no-push, creates branch/PR when changes detected)
  - Dispatch + automerge mode (calls bump with correct args)
  - Dispatch + PR mode (calls bump --no-push, creates branch/PR)
  - Release creation (iterates tags, calls gh release create)
  - No-op when on_merge=false and event=push
- [ ] `.semver/config.json` updated: add `bump-and-release` to `files` array (triggers auto patch bump on changes)

title: Slim version-bump.yml workflow and update install.sh
body:
## Summary

Replace the logic-heavy `version-bump.yml` workflow with a thin shell that delegates to `.semver/bump-and-release`. Update `install.sh` to deliver the new script. Update the template workflow.

## Changes

### 1. `templates/github/workflows/version-bump.yml`
The template workflow becomes a thin shell:
- Keep: name, triggers, permissions, concurrency, checkout step, job-level `if` for skip conditions
- Replace: all inline bash steps → single step that sets env vars and calls `.semver/bump-and-release`
- The workflow passes GitHub-specific context (event.before, inputs) as env vars

### 2. `.github/workflows/version-bump.yml` (this repo's own copy)
Update to match the new template. This repo dogfoods itself so both must be consistent.

### 3. `install.sh`
Add download of `bump-and-release` script alongside `git-semver`:
```bash
fetch_file "bump-and-release" ".semver/bump-and-release"
chmod +x .semver/bump-and-release
```

### 4. Vendor protection
Update `.vendored/config.json` if needed — the `.semver/**` glob already covers `.semver/bump-and-release`, so no change may be needed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `templates/github/workflows/version-bump.yml` is a thin workflow (~40 lines) that calls `.semver/bump-and-release`
- [ ] `.github/workflows/version-bump.yml` matches the template
- [ ] `install.sh` downloads and installs `bump-and-release` alongside `git-semver`
- [ ] Workflow passes all required env vars: `GITHUB_EVENT_BEFORE`, `INPUT_BUMP_TYPE`, `INPUT_SUBDIRECTORY`, `INPUT_CHANGELOG_DESCRIPTION`
- [ ] Built-in GHA vars (`GITHUB_EVENT_NAME`, `GITHUB_SHA`, `GITHUB_REPOSITORY`, `GH_TOKEN`) are available without explicit passing
- [ ] `.vendored/config.json` protection still covers the new script (`.semver/**` glob)
- [ ] No functional regression — the new workflow + script produces identical behavior to the old workflow

title: Update README for script-driven bump-and-release architecture
body:
## Summary

Update documentation to reflect the new architecture where `version-bump.yml` delegates to `.semver/bump-and-release`.

## Changes

### README.md
- Document the new `.semver/bump-and-release` script in the architecture section
- Update any references to workflow logic to point to the script instead
- Note that workflows are installed once and updates are delivered via scripts

### templates/AGENTS.md
- Add `.semver/bump-and-release` to the file permissions table (DO NOT edit — vendored)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] README.md describes `.semver/bump-and-release` and its role
- [ ] README.md explains the "install once, update via scripts" model
- [ ] `templates/AGENTS.md` file permissions table includes bump-and-release
- [ ] No stale references to inline workflow logic remain

title: Add _vendor self-registration to config template
body:
Follow-up to gsv-9ed5 (Vendor V2 Contract Compliance).

When install.sh creates .vendored/configs/git-semver.json on first install, it fetches templates/semver/config.json which lacks _vendor metadata. The framework needs this block for protected/allowed path enforcement.

Update templates/semver/config.json to include:

```json
"_vendor": {
  "repo": "mangimangi/git-semver",
  "install_branch": "chore/install-git-semver",
  "protected": [".vendored/pkg/git-semver/**", ".github/workflows/version-bump.yml"],
  "allowed": [".vendored/configs/git-semver.json"]
}
```

Acceptance criteria:
- templates/semver/config.json includes _vendor block with correct protected/allowed paths
- No sed/substitution needed — all values are static
- Existing repos unaffected (create-only guard in install.sh)
- Delete docs/planning/vendor-self-registration.md after implementation (knowledge migrated to issue)

title: vendor-config-validation: git-semver
body: Add schema file declaring git-semver's owned config fields. Planning doc: docs/planning/epics/gsv-f0fc.md

title: Config split: create pearls.yaml and madreperla.yaml, clean up medici.yaml
body:
Part of medici config split (mdci-23b1 phase 3).

Create pearls.yaml (prefix: gsv, eval.threshold: 80) and madreperla.yaml (description, docs, providers) at repo root. Remove flat-field identity config from medici.yaml. Clean up .vendored/configs/pearls.json if it duplicates config. Verify prl and madp resolve config correctly.

Source planning doc: medici/docs/orchestration/config-split/git-semver.md

title: Workflow template hardcodes .vendored/pkg/git-semver/ path
body:
version-bump.yml template references .vendored/pkg/git-semver/release but VENDOR_INSTALL_DIR controls where scripts are installed. If VENDOR_INSTALL_DIR differs from .vendored/pkg/git-semver/, the workflow breaks at runtime.

Fix: install.sh should interpolate INSTALL_DIR into the workflow template when copying to .github/workflows/. Either sed the path at install time, or generate the workflow from a template with the path substituted.
